steps.title: Identify what needs to happen
steps.content: By 367 BCE, Sidonian coins had fallen to 74.2% silver — below the 80% trade threshold — causing a confidence crisis. Abd‘ashtart I needed to raise the silver percentage above 80% without adding more silver (Sidon was under heavy financial pressure). The key insight: keeping the amount of silver constant while reducing the coin’s total weight automatically raises the percentage of silver.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the right one
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'proclaim that the percentage of silver in coins suitable for trade would be raised to a threshold higher than 80%.': Raising the threshold would make the situation worse, not better — it would disqualify even more coins. ✗
  • Choice B — 'keep the amount of silver in Sidonian coins consistent with that in coins minted in 367 BCE but decrease their weight.': Same silver mass in a lighter coin → higher silver percentage → coin clears the 80% threshold and restores trade-suitability. ✓
  • Choice C — 'begin minting heavier coins with a proportion of silver to copper similar to that in coins minted in 367 BCE.': Keeping the same ratio (~74% silver) in a heavier coin still leaves coins below 80% — confidence is not restored. ✗
  • Choice D — 'fund the mining of some copper deposits that were not available to Ba’alšillem II.': More copper is the opposite of what’s needed; the problem was too much copper making coins unsuitable for trade. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
